marketState: neutral, near-term bullish tilt on hot weather vs longer-term storage overhang
summary: Front price ($2.916) is sitting almost exactly at model fair value ($2.923), but the fair-value curve climbs to $3.028 by +21D while spot has been flat, implying a modest upward drift if the seasonal pattern holds. Near-term (1-7D) and medium-term (8-16D) weather-driven demand are running +14.5% and +11.4% above the 5-year norm respectively, both in the upper third of their ranges, which is a genuine bullish physical driver and lines up with the front trading at a slight premium to next month (mild backwardation). Working against that: storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average and this week's build beat forecast (bearish surprise), price is down 14.4% YTD versus a -6.9% median (weak year), and the 16-year seasonality history gives only a 38% chance of higher prices 3-4 weeks out with low reliability (59pp spread). Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, an extreme positioning that raises squeeze risk if heat persists, but could also just reflect a well-entrenched bear trend. Technicals and the futures curve dataset are empty in this snapshot, so there is no price-action or curve-shape confirmation beyond the raw front/next spread.

longTerm.rationale: Genuinely two-sided setup: rising fair-value trajectory, hot multi-week weather, and an extreme-short COT position all argue for upside risk, while a growing +180 Bcf storage surplus with a bearish build surprise and weak/unreliable seasonality (38% odds higher in 3-4wks, Low reliability) argue for caution on the long side.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for confluence: either a pullback toward the fair-value band's lower half (~$2.55-$2.65) alongside stabilizing/shrinking storage surprises (confirms LONG), or a clean break below ~$2.70 with continued bearish storage surprises and no MM short-covering (confirms SHORT continuation).

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk (risk ~$0.10/MMBtu; size contracts so that equals ~1% of account equity)
shortTerm.rationale: Current and 8-16D weather demand both running well above the 5y norm (+14.5%/+11.4%, upper-range percentiles), front trading at a slight premium to next month (mild backwardation), and price sitting at fair value now with the fair estimate rising toward $2.941 by +7D give a modest bullish edge for a short-dated trade.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close back below $2.80 or a rollover in the 1-7D weather demand reading toward/below the 5y norm would invalidate the setup.

keyDrivers: Weather demand +14.5% (1-7D) / +11.4% (8-16D) vs 5y, both in upper third of range — strong cooling/power-burn support
Fair value rising from $2.923 (now) to $3.028 (+21D) while front price has been flat — bullish drift bias
Storage surplus +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish build surprise (+22 Bcf vs +19 forecast) — bearish supply overhang
Managed-money net short at just the 8th percentile of 5y range — crowded short, squeeze risk if heat persists

risks: Technicals dataset is empty and futures curve data unavailable in this snapshot — no price-action or curve-shape confirmation beyond the raw front/next spread
